title: Merge layout + enrichment: preserve curated data across reindex
context: replaceAll was treating the fresh scan as the complete source of truth, discarding enrichment (responsibility, owners, status, fragility, provenance) on every reindex. This caused both silent data loss and git churn from timestamp mutations even when layout unchanged.
decision: Split component semantics: layout facts (paths, kind, name) come from the fresh scan; enrichment (curation, synthesis metadata) is loaded from prior records and merged forward. Timestamps preserved so unchanged components are byte-identical. Only updated_at advances if actual content changed (detected by normalized JSON comparison).
consequences: Enrichment survives reindexing — human curation and synthesis tools' output no longer lost
Deterministic: unchanged layout → byte-identical record, zero git churn
updated_at only advances on real changes, enabling accurate dirty-detection
Merge logic now carries precedence rules (e.g., prefer prior provenance if not 'inferred'; max fragility; preserve owners if nonempty)
alternatives_rejected: Store enrichment separately: couples interdependent records, complicates queries
Keep all prior fields: stale layout data persists after structure changes
Detect changes via timestamps: fragile to clock skew, doesn't prevent loss
Back up enrichment before reindex: doesn't prevent loss, only recovery

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Store enrichment separately: couples interdependent records, complicates queries
Keep all prior fields: stale layout data persists after structure changes
Detect changes via timestamps: fragile to clock skew, doesn't prevent loss
Back up enrichment before reindex: doesn't prevent loss, only recovery
